verses.commentary: Here is a trustworthy saying marks another credal statement. Whoever aspires to be an overseer desires a noble task affirms leadership aspiration as legitimate ambition. Overseer (episkopos) describes function: watching over the flock.

Now the overseer is to be above reproach heads the qualification list. Faithful to his wife (literally: one-woman man) requires sexual fidelity or singleness of devotion. Temperate, self-controlled, respectable, hospitable, able to teach form character and skill requirements. Teaching ability is the unique functional skill.

Not given to drunkenness, not violent but gentle, not quarrelsome, not a lover of money lists disqualifications. Elder must not be enslaved to wine, prone to violence, contentious, or greedy. Character over charisma.

He must manage his own family well tests leadership at home. And see that his children obey him requires effective parenting. In a manner worthy of full respect qualifies the obedience: authority exercised with dignity, not tyranny. Home proves fitness for church leadership.

If anyone does not know how to manage his own family, how can he take care of God's church? argues from lesser to greater. Family precedes church chronologically and logically. Failed home leadership disqualifies church oversight.

He must not be a recent convert warns against premature elevation. Or he may become conceited identifies the danger: pride. And fall under the same judgment as the devil links pride to Satan's fall. Time tests character and guards against inflation.

He must also have a good reputation with outsiders requires external validation. So that he will not fall into disgrace and into the devil's trap explains purpose: protection from scandal and satanic accusation. Public testimony matters.

In the same way, deacons transitions to second office. Worthy of respect, sincere, not indulging in much wine, and not pursuing dishonest gain lists character requirements similar to overseers but without teaching requirement.

They must keep hold of the deep truths of the faith with a clear conscience demands doctrinal and moral integrity. Deep truths (literally: the mystery) suggests full gospel understanding. Clear conscience links belief to behavior.

They must first be tested requires probation period. And then if there is nothing against them, let them serve as deacons makes serving contingent on examination. Testing precedes installation; character must be demonstrated before formal recognition.

In the same way, the women likely refers to deacons' wives or female deacons. Worthy of respect, not malicious talkers but temperate and trustworthy in everything sets standards parallel to male deacons. Women's critical role in ministry receives acknowledgment and regulation.

A deacon must be faithful to his wife parallels verse 2. And must manage his children and his household well repeats the domestic requirement. Family faithfulness qualifies for church service across all offices.

Those who have served well gain an excellent standing promises reward. And great assurance in their faith in Christ Jesus adds spiritual confidence. Faithful service produces both reputation and inner boldness.

Although I hope to come to you soon indicates Paul's travel plans. I am writing you these instructions explains the letter's purpose despite hoped-for visit. Written instruction provides stability during absence.

If I am delayed, you will know how people ought to conduct themselves in God's household addresses contingency. Which is the church of the living God identifies church as God's family and God as living, not dead like idols. The pillar and foundation of the truth assigns to church the role of supporting and displaying truth, not creating it.

Beyond all question, the mystery from which true godliness springs is great introduces a hymn or creed. He appeared in the flesh (incarnation), was vindicated by the Spirit (resurrection), was seen by angels (cosmic witness), was preached among the nations (mission), was believed on in the world (conversion), was taken up in glory (ascension) traces Christ's redemptive trajectory in six movements.
